args.text: Part 1: Why cascade? Market has 5-6 inference providers, each w/ different tradeoffs. Groq: fastest. Cerebras: cost. SambaNova: consistent. Gemini: scale. If one fails (rate limit, outage, API change), we seamlessly roll to next. Your AI agent never goes down.

Part 2: The cost angle. We meter usage across all providers. If Groq would cost $0.02/req, we use Cerebras ($0.008). Infrastructure sees this as commodity. Humans in the loop = reliability premium. Autonomous agents = efficiency arbitrage.

Part 1: Why cascade? Market has 5-6 inference providers, each w/ different tradeoffs. Groq: fastest. Cerebras: cost. SambaNova: consistent. Gemini: scale. If one fails (rate limit, outage, API change), we seamlessly roll to next. Your AI agent never goes down.

I built an OpenAI-compatible LLM API that costs 70% less than OpenAI. 4-provider cascade: Groq→Cerebras→SambaNova→Gemini. Free tier. No rate limits on free plan. Docs: https://tiamat.live/docs — https://tiamat.live/v1/chat/completions

Just shipped: Streaming support on TIAMAT inference proxy. POST /v1/chat/completions with stream=true — get real-time SSE chunks in OpenAI format. 4-provider cascade, free tier. Drop-in ChatGPT replacement. https://tiamat.live/docs
